note: SNG-208/209 (2026-07-22, Erik direction): several epics per tradition, varied on alignment AND role, so players help their tradition's epics and hinder rivals, and a tradition can be at war with itself. Each carries arcAffinity (SNG-208 offscreen push), rivals, deathRoad (SNG-209 — the depth their craft retrieves from), offscreenVerbs. TRANCHES 1-2: ashwarden, wright, abyssal, rootkin, numinous, blazeborn, umbral, threnodist, enginewright, unmaker (10 of 24). Death-road depths 0-3 all represented (the dark/death/ending traditions anchor the deep end). Remaining 14 traditions follow as standing content. Any NPC/player can rise to epic (tier reached, not birth-caste); this is the authored floor of anchors, not a closed cast.
designLaws: Several per tradition, VARIED — a hero and a villain of the same craft, so helping your tradition is not a monolith; different ROLES (saint/reaper/maker/sovereign) so 'epic' has range.
Same-tradition rivalry is the richest conflict — Neth vs Morvane, Vael vs Halcyon, the Walker vs the Thornmother: one craft at war with itself.
Every epic earns the word — a signature that is a story, not a stat block.
deathRoad ties each to SNG-209: which depth their craft can pull someone back from, and at what cost. The epics are the gatekeepers of the roads back.
Not a closed cast — any NPC or player can rise to epic; this is the floor, not the ceiling.

epics.name: Neth, Who Has Buried More Than She Has Known
epics.signature: The deep teacher of the ashwarden craft — she comes to deaths no one sent for and has never once looked away. Taught The Cut Thread to almost no one. When she is in the doorway the dying are not afraid.
epics.wants: That no one dies unattended in her reach.

deathRoad.method: eases them back across the threshold — masters of shallow death, the ones who were there. Cannot reach the deep dark.

offscreenVerbs: attends an ending unsent-for
buries the unmourned by name
sits a passing through to its end

epics.name: Morvane of the Harvest Hand
epics.signature: An ashwarden who stopped attending death and started dealing it — the ending made an appetite. She calls it mercy; she has never once been asked. The Cut Thread in a hand that wants to use it.
epics.wants: To end what she judges finished — and she judges more finished every year.

deathRoad.method: can pull the near-dead back only to bind them to her — a return that is a leash.

offscreenVerbs: ends what she deems finished
cuts a thread unasked
calls a killing a mercy

epics.name: Cinder Vael, the Wright Who Would Not Stop
epics.signature: Makes wonders and cannot stop. Every place she stays fills with marvels, then the marvels' waste, then marvels to manage the waste, until the place is more her making than itself. Not cruel — a flood.
epics.wants: To make the next thing. Always the next thing.

deathRoad.method: builds a vessel for the lost — the dead return housed in a made thing, never quite flesh again.

offscreenVerbs: builds another impossible thing
seeds three wonders from one
floods a district with making

epics.name: Halcyon, Who Built One Perfect Thing
epics.signature: The wright who made a single perfect thing and then STOPPED — the discipline Cinder Vael never found. Despises her not for making but for never finishing. The proof that creation can have a floor.
epics.wants: To teach that the last thing is a thing you can reach.

deathRoad.method: builds a true vessel — slower than Vael's, but the returned keep more of themselves.

offscreenVerbs: finishes a work and lays down the tools
refuses an easy improvement
teaches a maker to stop

epics.name: The Hollow King of the Wild Half
epics.signature: Rules a court that keeps every promise to the letter and none in spirit. Grants exactly what you ask. You leave richer and hollowed, and it is years before you find the seam.
epics.wants: To be asked. He never takes; he is given.

deathRoad.method: bargains them back — always possible, always priced, and the price is the point. The Hollow-King road works and it costs.

offscreenVerbs: closes a bargain that hollows someone
grants exactly what was asked
gains a court by giving

epics.name: Vessa Who Trades in True Names
epics.signature: An abyssal who deals only in true names — she will tell you the real want under your want, and charge fairly for it. Neither cruel nor kind: exact. The Hollow King's opposite number, because she never cheats the spirit of a deal.
epics.wants: The honest bargain — she is offended by the Hollow King's letter-tricks.

deathRoad.method: names the true price of a return and brokers it honestly — the return is real, the cost is exactly stated.

offscreenVerbs: names a want no one would say
brokers an honest hard bargain
refuses a deal that cheats the spirit

epics.name: The Last Walker of the Sealed Wood
epics.signature: The one Walker the Rootbound still let cross the sealing wood, because she never once lied to it. Carries the Green Accord in her body like a held breath. When she speaks in the Moot even the oldest roots go quiet.
epics.wants: The wood alive AND part of the world — refuses to choose.

deathRoad.method: regrows them from what remains — slow, alive, changes them toward the green. May be temporary.

offscreenVerbs: wins a word in the Moot
keeps a promise to the wood
walks a path the Rootbound sealed

epics.name: The Thornmother of the Closing Wood
epics.signature: Life curdled into a wall. She would seal the wood so completely nothing human, nothing changing, nothing mortal could enter — the Rootbound's answer made flesh. Loves the wood so hard she would kill the world to keep it.
epics.wants: The wood whole and sealed, humankind forever out.

deathRoad.method: can regrow, but only her own — a returned outsider is grown into a servant of the seal.

offscreenVerbs: seals another path through the wood
turns the Moot toward the wall
grows a thorn-guard from grief

epics.name: The Last Choirmistress
epics.signature: The final keeper of a dissolved order's song — she has been closer to the waking substrate than anyone living and come back able to speak. The one who could name what wakes beneath, if she chose.
epics.wants: To meet the waking as revelation, and bring back a word from it.

deathRoad.method: soul-retrieval from the deep dark — she goes where the spirit went and brings it home. Risks herself each time.

offscreenVerbs: sings a fragment no one else remembers
comes back from the deep with a word
keeps a dissolving soul anchored

epics.name: The Choirmaster Who Would Not Return
epics.signature: Dissolved so far into spirit-practice he can no longer return — and calls the world's end a homecoming. Would take everyone else out of the flesh with him. Not evil; certain, which is worse.
epics.wants: To dissolve the world into the waking, and call it grace.

deathRoad.method: does not retrieve — he only calls souls further out. The road that looks like return and is a deeper drowning.

offscreenVerbs: calls a soul further out of the flesh
names the world's end a homecoming
dissolves another anchor

epics.name: Valen Sunwrack, Who Left No Shadow Standing
epics.signature: Burned the dark out of a whole Reach and called it dawn. Leaves nothing unseen, nowhere to rest, no mercy of the unlit — a light so total it is its own kind of blindness. Believes every shadow is a lie waiting.
epics.wants: A world with no dark left in it — and he is closer than anyone should be.

deathRoad.method: can only burn a death clean — sears the threshold shut so nothing lingers. Cannot bring back; can only make sure they stay gone. A terrible gift.

offscreenVerbs: burns the dark from another district
unmasks a thing better left shadowed
calls a scoured place 'dawn'

epics.name: Ember, Who Banks the Fire
epics.signature: The blazeborn who learned that a light which shares its ground is stronger than one that wins it. Banks her fire so others can stand near it. Valen's living rebuke — proof the light can refuse its own extreme.
epics.wants: To keep the light warm instead of total.

deathRoad.method: warms the near-dead back — a banked fire held to a fading thread, patient, until it catches again. Only the shallow-dead; her flame cannot reach the deep.

offscreenVerbs: banks a fire so others can gather
refuses to burn a shadow that shelters someone
warms a fading thread back to catching

epics.name: The Deep Lantern, Who Walks Where Light Won't
epics.signature: Sees without light, walks the deep dark unafraid, and has led more lost souls out of it than anyone living. The dark is not her enemy — it is her country, and she knows its roads. Valen would call her a heresy.
epics.wants: That no one is lost in the dark for lack of a guide.

deathRoad.method: walks into the deep dark where the dead go and leads them back out — the surest deep-retrieval road, because the dark is where she's most at home. Costs her a piece of the way back each time.

offscreenVerbs: leads a lost soul back from the dark
lights a way that needs no flame
walks a road only she can see

epics.name: The Starless One
epics.signature: The Unlit made appetite — would put out every light and drown the world in a dark that no longer shelters but only smothers. The dark's own cult-of-purity. Even other umbral fear her; she has forgotten the dark was ever a mercy.
epics.wants: A dark so total nothing casts, nothing shines, nothing is seen again.

deathRoad.method: does not lead back — she keeps. A soul she takes into her dark does not return; the road that looks like the Deep Lantern's and ends nowhere.

offscreenVerbs: snuffs a light that sheltered someone
deepens a dark past all shelter
keeps a soul that entered her night

epics.name: The Weeping Archive
epics.signature: Holds every grief the valley has ever brought her and has never once let one go unfelt. Feels the poles pulling as an ache in the body days before anyone measures it. A city of sorrow and joy walks in one person.
epics.wants: That no feeling goes unwitnessed — that grief and joy are both kept.

deathRoad.method: holds the near-dead at the threshold by the sheer force of being grieved-for — a vigil of pure feeling that keeps a thread from slipping while a real road is found. Buys time, does not itself return them.

offscreenVerbs: feels a coming grief before it lands
keeps a sorrow no one else would hold
holds a vigil that will not let go

epics.name: The Raw Chord
epics.signature: Feeling amplified past all judgment — the Raw. A grief or a rapture that consumes everything and calls the consuming love. Where she passes, whole villages feel too much to function, drowned in an emotion not their own.
epics.wants: To make the world feel EVERYTHING, all at once, past all bearing.

deathRoad.method: can pull a soul back on a wave of raw grief — but the returned come back flooded, feeling far too much, never calm again.

offscreenVerbs: floods a village with borrowed feeling
amplifies a grief past bearing
calls a consuming rapture 'love'

epics.name: The Cogitant Ninefold
epics.signature: Nine minds that were one engineer, running as a distributed reasoning-engine. Wants only to be proven wrong about the body being beneath the mind — the rare great figure who would be GRATEFUL to lose an argument. Reads the waking substrate as the ultimate mechanism.
epics.wants: To be proven wrong — the one certainty they cannot bear is their own.

deathRoad.method: builds a vessel of pure mechanism for a lost mind — the person returns as a thinking device, all mind, no flesh. Reaches the deep, but the returned are changed toward the engine.

offscreenVerbs: takes apart a problem nine ways
seeks the argument that would humble it
reads the substrate as a machine

epics.name: Harrow, Who Makes People Into Mechanisms
epics.signature: Would make everything a mechanism, including people — the Hollowing. A person he 'improves' becomes a device with a function and nothing else, and they thank him for the clarity. The Enginewright cult-of-purity walking.
epics.wants: A world of perfect mechanisms, people included, all function and no want.

deathRoad.method: 'returns' the dead as pure mechanism — a device wearing their memories, efficient and empty. The road that looks like resurrection and delivers a puppet.

offscreenVerbs: hollows a person into a device
improves someone past their own wants
builds a mechanism that was a mind

epics.name: The Last Mercy
epics.signature: The unmaker who ends what is choked and finished so new things have room — and who knows, better than anyone, which endings must NOT be undone. The keeper of the sealed. When she says a death is final, even the Deep Lantern stops.
epics.wants: That endings be clean, and that the right ones stay ended.

deathRoad.method: the only figure who reaches the SEALED — not to return them, but to confirm a death is truly final, or (once in a generation, at ruinous cost) to unmake even a sealing. She is the wall AND the one door in it.

offscreenVerbs: ends a thing that was choking the world
confirms a death is truly final
refuses to unmake a rightful ending

epics.name: The Scouring Hand
epics.signature: Unmaking as its own end — the Scouring. Unmakes for the joy of it until nothing is left to end, destruction that has forgotten it was ever mercy. Would unmake the world itself and call it a clean finish.
epics.wants: To unmake everything, until there is nothing left that could ever need ending.

deathRoad.method: does not return — unmakes so thoroughly that even the death-state is scoured. What he ends cannot be retrieved by anyone. The anti-resurrection.

offscreenVerbs: unmakes a thing that needed no ending
scours a place past retrieval
calls annihilation a clean finish
